sections.paragraph:
& אֶתְמוּל once אִתְּמֹול 1Sa 10:11 i.q. תְּמֹול with Aleph prosthetic, adv.

(1) yesterday, 1Sa 4:7 14:21 19:7 Psa 90:4.

(2) formerly, used generally of time long past, Mic 2:8 Isa 30:33. The same form is also found in Syr., Ch., and in Cod. Nasar. [“There exists likewise a form תְּמֹול, which see. Also, Syr. ܐܶܬܡܠܳ; Chald. אִתְמֹול, אִתְמָלֵי. The form seems compounded from אֵת with, at, and מֹול i.q. פָּנִים forepart, front; hence of time, antea, aforetime.” Ges. add.]
